terms.definition: The lower jawbone, the only movable bone of the skull, articulating with the temporal bone at the TMJ.
terms.exampleSentence: Palpation of the mandible revealed tenderness over the angle on the right side.
terms.contextNotes: Adjective form 'mandibular' is used for nerves and arches.

terms.definition: The upper jawbone, forming the hard palate and supporting the upper dental arch.
terms.exampleSentence: The radiograph showed a cystic lesion in the left maxilla adjacent to the canine.
terms.contextNotes: Adjective: maxillary. Plural: maxillae.

terms.definition: The soft tissue (gum) surrounding the teeth and covering the alveolar bone.
terms.exampleSentence: The gingiva appeared erythematous and bled on gentle probing.
terms.contextNotes: Plural: gingivae. Adjective: gingival.

terms.definition: The supporting tissues of the tooth: gingiva, periodontal ligament, cementum, and alveolar bone.
terms.exampleSentence: Chronic inflammation of the periodontium leads to attachment loss and tooth mobility.
terms.contextNotes: Adjective: periodontal. Plural: periodontia.

terms.definition: The hard, mineralised outer layer of the tooth crown; the hardest tissue in the human body.
terms.exampleSentence: Erosion had thinned the enamel on the palatal surfaces of the upper incisors.
terms.contextNotes: Acellular and cannot regenerate once lost.

terms.definition: The yellowish, mineralised tissue beneath enamel and cementum, forming the bulk of the tooth.
terms.exampleSentence: Once caries reaches the dentine, progression is typically more rapid.
terms.contextNotes: Contains tubules transmitting pain stimuli to the pulp.

terms.definition: The soft connective tissue at the centre of the tooth containing nerves, blood vessels, and lymphatics.
terms.exampleSentence: The deep cavity had exposed the pulp, necessitating root canal therapy.
terms.contextNotes: Adjective: pulpal.

terms.definition: The thin calcified tissue covering the tooth root and providing attachment for the periodontal ligament.
terms.exampleSentence: Root scaling was performed without removing healthy cementum.
terms.contextNotes: Less mineralised than enamel and dentine.

terms.definition: The portion of the maxilla and mandible that contains and supports the tooth sockets.
terms.exampleSentence: Radiographs revealed generalised horizontal alveolar bone loss.
terms.contextNotes: Resorbs after tooth extraction if not preserved.

terms.definition: Temporomandibular joint: the synovial joint between the mandible and the temporal bone of the skull.
terms.exampleSentence: The patient reported clicking and pain in the right TMJ on opening.
terms.contextNotes: Often abbreviated TMD for the disorder.

terms.definition: The contact relationship between the maxillary and mandibular teeth when the jaws close.
terms.exampleSentence: Following the restoration, the occlusion was checked with articulating paper.
terms.contextNotes: Adjective: occlusal (e.g., occlusal surface).

terms.definition: The arrangement, type, and number of teeth in the dental arches at a given developmental stage.
terms.exampleSentence: The child presented with mixed dentition and a retained deciduous canine.
terms.contextNotes: Mixed dentition typically occurs ages 6–12.

terms.definition: The first set of 20 teeth that erupt in childhood and are later shed; primary teeth.
terms.exampleSentence: All deciduous teeth had exfoliated by the age of twelve.
terms.contextNotes: Use 'baby teeth' when speaking with parents.

terms.definition: The 32 adult teeth that succeed and supplement the deciduous dentition.
terms.exampleSentence: The first permanent molars typically erupt at around six years of age.
terms.contextNotes: Includes incisors, canines, premolars, molars.

terms.definition: Any of the eight chisel-shaped anterior teeth used for cutting food.
terms.exampleSentence: A composite restoration was placed on the fractured upper central incisor.
terms.contextNotes: Numbered 1 and 2 in each quadrant.

terms.definition: The pointed tooth situated between incisors and premolars; used for tearing food.
terms.exampleSentence: The upper canine was buccally displaced and required orthodontic alignment.
terms.contextNotes: Often the last anterior tooth to erupt.

terms.definition: Either of the two transitional teeth between the canine and the molars in each quadrant.
terms.exampleSentence: The upper first premolar was extracted to relieve crowding.
terms.contextNotes: Two roots common in upper first premolar.

terms.definition: The large posterior teeth used for grinding food; three per quadrant in adults.
terms.exampleSentence: Deep occlusal caries was noted in the lower right first molar.
terms.contextNotes: Third molars are commonly impacted.

terms.definition: The most posterior molar in each quadrant, commonly known as the wisdom tooth.
terms.exampleSentence: Surgical removal of the impacted lower third molar was scheduled.
terms.contextNotes: Often erupts between ages 17 and 25.

terms.definition: The fibrous connective tissue attaching cementum of the root to the alveolar bone.
terms.exampleSentence: The periodontal ligament was widened, suggesting occlusal trauma.
terms.contextNotes: Critical for tooth proprioception and shock absorption.

terms.definition: The bony anterior portion of the roof of the mouth, formed by the maxillae and palatine bones.
terms.exampleSentence: A small ulcer was visible on the hard palate behind the incisors.
terms.contextNotes: Distinct from the soft palate posteriorly.

terms.definition: The muscular posterior portion of the roof of the mouth, ending in the uvula.
terms.exampleSentence: Erythema of the soft palate suggested viral pharyngitis.
terms.contextNotes: Important in swallowing and speech.

terms.definition: The muscular organ in the floor of the mouth, essential for taste, mastication, swallowing, and speech.
terms.exampleSentence: Lateral borders of the tongue were inspected for suspicious lesions.
terms.contextNotes: Adjective: lingual.

terms.definition: A small fold of mucous membrane that limits the movement of an organ such as the tongue or lip.
terms.exampleSentence: A short lingual frenulum was contributing to restricted tongue movement.
terms.contextNotes: Frenectomy may be indicated for tongue-tie.

terms.definition: Any of the exocrine glands that produce saliva and secrete it into the oral cavity.
terms.exampleSentence: Swelling of the parotid salivary gland was noted on the right side.
terms.contextNotes: Three major paired glands: parotid, submandibular, sublingual.

terms.definition: The largest salivary gland, located anterior and inferior to the ear, draining via Stensen's duct.
terms.exampleSentence: Mumps typically presents with bilateral parotid gland enlargement.
terms.contextNotes: Facial nerve passes through it.

terms.definition: The bony ridge of the maxilla or mandible that contains the tooth sockets.
terms.exampleSentence: The edentulous alveolar ridge had undergone significant resorption.
terms.contextNotes: Critical landmark for denture design.

terms.definition: A pointed or rounded projection on the chewing surface of a tooth.
terms.exampleSentence: A vertical fracture extended through the palatal cusp of the upper molar.
terms.contextNotes: Adjective: cuspal.

terms.definition: The portion of the tooth embedded in the alveolar bone, covered by cementum.
terms.exampleSentence: Periapical radiograph confirmed a fracture of the mesial root.
terms.contextNotes: Number of roots varies by tooth type.

terms.definition: The portion of the tooth visible above the gum line, covered by enamel.
terms.exampleSentence: The clinical crown of the tooth was severely worn from bruxism.
terms.contextNotes: Distinguish anatomical crown from prosthetic crown restoration.
